title: spergula pentandra subsp. morisonii
intro: Spergula pentandra subsp. morisonii, also known as Spear-leaved sandwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Spergula pentandra subsp. morisonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in water, and it is often used in teas, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
